defs: -ism (in Chinese, a prefix, often combined with a suffix such as 主義|主义[zhu3 yi4] or 論|论[lun4], e.g. 唯理論|唯理论[wei2 li3 lun4], rationalism)
(slang) (adjective) (of one thing) one of the only two; (of two things) the only two
(idiom) to be bent solely on profit; to put profit before everything else
philosophy of idealism, the doctrine that external reality is a product of consciousness
philosophy of idealism, the doctrine that external reality is a product of consciousness

pinyin: wei2 kong3 tian1 xia4 bu4 luan4
defs: to wish for the whole world to be in chaos (idiom)
metaphysical view, esp. due to Schopenhauer 叔本華|叔本华[Shu1 ben3 hua2], that the essence of the world is willpower
materialism, philosophical doctrine that physical matter is the whole of reality
fan who only likes one particular member of a pop idol band
